titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Anne Seymour Damer Collection
titlestmt.author: by Lewis Walpole Library Staff

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday March 23, 2024 at 01:00

descriptivenote.p: Chiefly in English; some material in French.

did.abstract: The collections holds letters to and from Anne Seymour Damer's friends and relatives, with a small group of documents in her hand.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: W. S. Lewis purchased most of the material at Christie's (Sir Wathen Arthur Waller sale, Lot 12), December 14, 1947. Bequest of Wilmarth Sheldon Lewis (Yale 1918), 1979.
Additional material was acquired from various sources; those acquisitions are noted at the folder level.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Letters to Damer, 1783-1823. II. Letters from Damer, 1793-1828. III. Other Papers, 1815-1828.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Anne Seymour Conway Damer, a sculptor and author, was born on November 8, 1749, at Coombe Bank, Sevenoaks, Kent, the only child of the military officer and politician Henry Seymour Conway (1719-1795) and his wife, Caroline Campbell Bruce, Lady Ailesbury (1721-1803). In 1767 she married the Hon. John Damer (1743-1776), but they separated seven years later. After his death she studied anatomy, modelling, and carving with the intention of becoming a sculptor; she was successful and exhibited her works at the Royal Academy over more than thirty years.

Damer was associated with British theatrical and literary circles, studied Greek and Latin, and published one novel, Belmour (1801). She traveled to Italy and France, including Florence and Naples, where she visited Sir William Hamilton and Paris where she presented sculptures to Napoleon. In London, Damer formed close friendships with several prominent women such as Mary Berry and Sarah Siddons. One of the greatest influences in her life and champions of her work was Horace Walpole (1717-1797) who was her guardian in her youth when her parents were abroad, and a promoter of her sculpture. Walpole made Damer his executor and bequeathed his home, Strawberry Hill in Twickenham, to her; she lived there from 1797 to 1811. Anne Seymour Damer died at her home in London on May 28, 1828.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Prior to February 2024 the material in LWL MSS 48 had been interfiled in the Lewis Walpole Library Manuscript Miscellany (formerly LWL MSS MISC, now LWL MSS 36).
The material in Box 1, Folders 1-29 was purchased by Wilmarth S. Lewis; all material in boxes and folders following those was purchased by the Lewis Walpole Library, Yale University.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Anne Seymour Damer Collection. The Lewis Walpole Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collections holds letters to and from Anne Seymour Damer's friends and relatives, with a small group of documents in her hand.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Anne Seymour Damer Collection is the physical property of the Lewis Walpole Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the W. S. Lewis Librarian/Executive Director.

persname.part: Cavendish, Georgiana Spencer, Duchess of Devonshire, 1757-1806
Richmond and Lennox, Charles Lennox, Duke of, 1735-1806

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letters in here are mostly single notes to Damer from a variety of friends, but there are multiple letters from some, such as the five from the Duchess of Devonshire and four from the Duke of Richmond, who wrote most often about his health. Among the interesting correspondents is Henry Richard Vassall, Baron Holland, who was sending proof pages from his edition of Horace Walpole's Memoires of the Last Ten Years of the Reign of George the Second (1822) that contain any mention of Damer's father Henry Seymour Conway "in case there should be any thing which you wish expunged." Also of note is Sir William Hamilton who, in his six-page letter wrote of "a chain of melancholy events" in Naples, featuring the deaths of his wife Catherine (putrid fever), Dr. Alexander Monro Drummond (a fractured skull after a fall from William Beckford's horse), and most dramatically, of the Princess Giraci Grimaldi, who was buried alive in her home in an earthquake and died of starvation.

did.unittitle: Caroline, Queen, consort of George IV, King of Great Britain, 1768-1821
Cavendish, Georgiana Spencer, Duchess of Devonshire, 1757-1806
Josephine, Empress, consort of Napoleon I, Emperor of the French, 1763-1814

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letter is not signed but the hand was identified by Jonathan Gross, editor of Byron's "Corbeau Blanc" : the Life and Letters of Lady Melbourne (1997)

did.unittitle: Richmond and Lennox, Charles Lennox, Duke of, 1735-1806

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The letters here are primarily personal, but three are to businessmen: one to James Christie, and two to the coachmakers Lukin and Allan. Among the personal letters are notes to the sculptor George Bullock inviting him to visit her in Strawberry Hill, writing that she "will, with pleasure, send a ticket" when he chooses the day, and to her cousin, Francis Ingram Seymour, Marquess of Hertford, regarding her father's papers; and a longer letter of thanks to the merchant/ancient languages scholar John Livie thanking him for getting her started with the study of Greek and reporting on her progress.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased at the Thomas Henry Foster Sale, lot 246.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from the James Marshall and Marie Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, 1998.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Winifred A. Myers, Ltd., May 1971.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from I. Kyrle Fletcher, March 1949.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: James Edwards, bookseller and bibliographer, married Katharine, the only daughter of Rev. Edward Bromhead, in September 1805.

did.unittitle: Hertford, Francis Ingram Seymour, Marquess of, 1743-1822

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Elkin Mathews Ltd., December 1943.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The 1804 letter was purchased from Peter Murray Hill, July 1947.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Winifred A. Myers, Ltd., February 1955.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Damer responds to an inquiry about her father's papers and references in them to Major General Lloyd.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Winifred A. Myers, Ltd., August 1958.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Damer thanks the recipient for the "flattering expressions and approbations you have honoured me with," and contrasts those against the "slights" she has experienced in her own country.

did.unittitle: Account of her presentation of a Charles Fox bust to Napoleon and Josephine in 1812
Biographical sketch with a list of her works
Inscription in the hand of Anne Seymour Damer

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Winifred A. Myers, Ltd., May 1971.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Text: Given by Her Majesty / The Empress Josephine / of France / To Anne Seymour Damer / 1811

did.unittitle: Statement appointing Jonathan Wathen Waller as her executor, signed
